# Releases

> Releases (releases.sh) is a changelog indexer and registry for developers and AI agents. This monorepo is the backend: it ingests changelogs from hundreds of vendors (GitHub releases, RSS feeds, scraped pages, App Store listings), stores them in Cloudflare D1, enriches them with AI (titles, summaries, classification, embeddings), and serves them over a public REST API, a hosted MCP server, and a Next.js web frontend.

This file is the entry point for an agent working in this repository. Read it first, then follow the links you need. Nothing here overrides `AGENTS.md` — that file is the canonical rulebook for working in this codebase; this one is the map.

## Orientation

- **Runtime:** Bun. **Language:** TypeScript (strict). **Infra:** Cloudflare Workers, D1, R2, KV, Vectorize, Workflows, Durable Objects. **Web:** Next.js on Vercel.
- **The API worker (`workers/api/`) is the only data plane.** Every read and write goes through it. The MCP worker, web frontend, CLI, and agents are all clients of it (or bind to the same D1).
- **The user-facing CLI is NOT in this repo.** It lives at [buildinternet/releases-cli](https://github.com/buildinternet/releases-cli) and talks to the API over HTTP. Schema and wire-type changes land here first (`packages/core`, `packages/api-types`), then the CLI bumps its npm pins.
- **Layout:** `workers/api` (Hono REST API on D1), `workers/mcp` (remote MCP server at mcp.releases.sh), `workers/discovery` (managed-agent session orchestrator), `workers/webhooks` (signed `release.created` delivery), `web/` (Next.js frontend), `packages/` (shared code), `src/agent/` + `src/shared/` (managed-agent prompts and tools), `.claude/` (skills, commands, workflows).

## Getting started

- Verify a change: `bun run check` (lint + format + type-check), then `bun test`. Evals (`tests/evals/`) are manual-only — they cost real API money.
- Local dev: `bun run dev:api` / `dev:web` / `dev:mcp` / `dev:discovery` serve on stable HTTPS subdomains (`https://releases.localhost`, `https://api.releases.localhost`, …). Secrets go in each worker's git-ignored `.dev.vars` (copy `.dev.vars.example`).
- Local DB broken or behind? `bun run db:reset:local` rebuilds it from migrations; `bun run db:pull` also imports a prod content subset (never auth tables).
- Full setup, deployment, and testing detail: [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md). Project conventions and per-package detail: [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md).

## Rules that always apply

These are the invariants most likely to bite an agent making a change. Full detail lives in the linked docs.

- **Errors:** every non-2xx API response is the nested envelope `{ error: { code, type, message, details? } }`. Throw a `ReleasesError` subclass; never hand-roll `c.json({ error })`. See [docs/architecture/errors.md](docs/architecture/errors.md).
- **Logging:** worker code uses `logEvent()` from `@releases/lib/log-event`; CLI and runtime-neutral packages use `@buildinternet/releases-lib/logger`. Never import the fs-backed logger into a worker. See [docs/architecture/logging.md](docs/architecture/logging.md).
- **D1 binds:** hard limit of 100 bound parameters per prepared statement. Batch inserts chunk accordingly; raising a chunk size without re-checking bind count causes 500s.
- **`source.url` is for humans.** Machine fetch endpoints (feed URLs, GitHub overrides, crawl flags) live in `source.metadata`. Test: would a human want to land on this URL? If no, it's metadata.
- **Feature flags are a last resort.** Ship features enabled with no flag unless you can name a concrete kill-switch, staged-rollout, or operational-lever need. See [docs/architecture/feature-flags.md](docs/architecture/feature-flags.md).
- **Safety gates fail closed.** Opt-out checks (robots.txt / Content-Signal) return the safe verdict on ambiguity, and a `no` is never overwritten by a later `yes`.
- **Route placement:** resource CRUD goes on `/v1/<resource>`, async job triggers on `/v1/workflows/<job>`, and only telemetry lives under `/v1/admin/*`. See [docs/architecture/routing.md](docs/architecture/routing.md).
- **`workers/mcp` must never import better-auth** (zod version pin). OAuth JWT verification there goes through `@releases/lib/oauth-jwt`.

## Architecture docs

Reference docs for shipped systems, in [docs/architecture/](docs/architecture/). Human-oriented reading paths: [docs/README.md](docs/README.md).

- [remote-mode.md](docs/architecture/remote-mode.md): The API worker itself — auth model (token lanes, OAuth, roles), rate limiting, caching, migrations, cron scheduling, and the SourceActor/OrgActor Durable Objects.
- [ingest.md](docs/architecture/ingest.md): How fetched content becomes release rows — adapter routing, dedup, backoff, URL exclusion, and the ingest-time AI passes.
- [routing.md](docs/architecture/routing.md): The REST route surface — naming buckets, entity resolution, the `/v1/lookups` resolvers, pagination shapes, and the OpenAPI coverage gate.
- [errors.md](docs/architecture/errors.md): The standardized error envelope — taxonomy, producers, consumers, and how to add a code.
- [logging.md](docs/architecture/logging.md): The per-runtime logging split and the auth audit-event table.
- [taxonomy.md](docs/architecture/taxonomy.md): The classification axes — `kind` vs `type` vs `category`, tags, release types, products, collections.
- [mcp.md](docs/architecture/mcp.md): The remote MCP server — tools, resources, prompts, auth and scope enforcement, App UIs, and the registry listing.
- [semantic-search.md](docs/architecture/semantic-search.md): Hybrid FTS5 + Vectorize search — indexes, ingest, recency ranking, query cache, related-entity rails.
- [agents.md](docs/architecture/agents.md): The Anthropic managed agents (discovery + worker) — deploys, tool surfaces, skills vs playbooks, and Claude Code integration.
- [web.md](docs/architecture/web.md): Web-facing features — URL resolution, changelog slicing, OG images, overviews, categories, collections, media pipeline, follows, admin hub.
- [events.md](docs/architecture/events.md): The release event bus — `ReleaseHub` Durable Object, the `/v1/releases/stream` WebSocket, and its consumers.
- [coverage.md](docs/architecture/coverage.md): Release coverage grouping (many posts, one launch) and cron observability.
- [extract.md](docs/architecture/extract.md): The two-tier AI extraction path — one-shot for small bodies, a tool-use loop for large ones.
- [firecrawl-monitoring.md](docs/architecture/firecrawl-monitoring.md): The external Firecrawl backend for challenge-blocked pages, its webhook diff format, and backfill/re-extract workflows.
- [local-ingest.md](docs/architecture/local-ingest.md): Onboarding a changelog from local Claude Code without the managed-agent bill, behind a mandatory opt-out preflight.
- [feature-flags.md](docs/architecture/feature-flags.md): The Cloudflare Flagship flag registry, evaluation order, and per-flag reference.
- [ai-gateway.md](docs/architecture/ai-gateway.md): The Cloudflare AI Gateway passthrough for Anthropic calls and the OpenRouter provider switch.
- [cli-distribution.md](docs/architecture/cli-distribution.md): How the out-of-tree CLI is published and which npm packages ship from which repo.
- [well-known-config.md](docs/architecture/well-known-config.md): Owner-declared `releases.json` listing metadata and its reconciliation rules.
- [workspaces.md](docs/architecture/workspaces.md): User-tenancy Workspaces (Better Auth org plugin) — distinct from the registry `organizations`.
- [consumption-telemetry.md](docs/architecture/consumption-telemetry.md): The PII-clean demand signal for MCP/API consumption.
- [maintenance-workspace.md](docs/architecture/maintenance-workspace.md): The `~/.releases/work/` audit-trail convention for agent-driven admin maintenance.

Design documents (not shipped systems — do not treat as current behavior):

- [durable-objects-exploration.md](docs/architecture/durable-objects-exploration.md): Design exploration for the per-entity actor architecture.
- [sourceactor-delegation-plan.md](docs/architecture/sourceactor-delegation-plan.md): Staged plan for routing scrape/agent delegation through SourceActor.

## Optional

- [Public llms.txt](https://releases.sh/llms.txt): The consumer-facing entry point for agents using the product (REST API, MCP server, CLI) rather than working in this repo. Served by `web/src/app/api/llms/route.ts`.
- [docs/webhooks.md](docs/webhooks.md): Public subscriber contract for outbound webhooks — headers, signature verification, retries.
- [docs/changelog-style.md](docs/changelog-style.md): Style guide for the project's own daily changelog.
- [docs/runbooks/](docs/runbooks/): Operational runbooks — auth-audit monitors, WAF rules, bot-auth registration, demand dashboard.
- [docs/superpowers/](docs/superpowers/): Historical design specs and plans referenced by the architecture docs.
- [.claude/skills/](.claude/skills/): The skill corpus shared by managed agents and local Claude Code.
